# General Purposes {#PurposeSection}

These guidelines compile the minimum data standards and recommended practices for the management and reporting of at-sea observation data under the IOTC Regional observer scheme (ROS) collected by onboard observers and Electronic Monitoring Systems (EMS). Their purpose is to ensure consistency, completeness, and quality of data submitted to the Secretariat of the Indian Ocean Tuna Commission (IOTC), in accordance with reporting obligations established under the ROS and relevant IOTC Conservation and Management Measures (CMMs). These guidelines are a resource tool for CPCs to consult before submitting ROS data. 

## Scope

The IOTC ROS was established to collect verified catch data and other scientific data related to the fisheries for tuna and tuna-like species in the IOTC Area of Competence, as defined in the IOTC Agreement. 

These guidelines apply to all CPCs operating vessels within the IOTC Area of Competence that are 24 meters length overall and above and under 24 meters if they operate outside the exclusive economic zone (EEZ). 

Flag States and CPCs are responsible for ensuring vessels flying their flag conduct fishing operations in accordance with IOTC CMMs. As part of these obligations, CPCs must ensure the required level of observer coverage, as specified in IOTC ROS CMM, is achieved and that observer-derived and EMS data are collected and reported to the IOTC Secretariat in accordance with the data standards, endorsed by the Scientific Committee. In the event of any inconsistency between these guidelines and recent adopted IOTC CMMs, the provisions of the CMMs shall prevail. 

Observer programmes are established and managed by CPCs, which are responsible for the recruitment, training, certification, deployment, and oversight of observers ([IOTC ROS Standards](https://iotc.org/sites/default/files/documents/ROS/Standards/IOTC_Regional_Observer_Scheme_Standards.pdf)). CPCs are responsible for ensuring that all observer data is submitted in accordance with the agreed upon IOTC data standards ([ROS Data Standards](https://iotc.org/sites/default/files/documents/ROS/Standards/Data_standard_ROS_fields.xlsx)). 

CPCs implementing EMS, are responsible for the installation, operation, maintenance, and management of EMS programs and must ensure that their electronic monitoring frameworks comply with the standards and requirements established under the IOTC Regional Electronic Monitoring Program (REMP), including technical specifications, data collection protocols, data review procedures ([ROS EMS program standards](https://iotc.org/sites/default/files/documents/ROS/Standards/Electronic_monitoring_program_standards.pdf)). CPCs are responsible for ensuring that EMS-derived data are reviewed, validated, and reported to the IOTC Secretariat in accordance with applicable IOTC reporting standards ([EMS system and data standards](https://iotc.org/sites/default/files/documents/ROS/Standards/Electronic_monitoring_system_and_data_standards.pdf)).  

Although there is a core CMM covering ROS requirements, other CMMs may include provisions relating to specific data collection requirements when are observers on board or EMS installed. The Secretariat periodically reviews these CMMs to ensure that the reporting requirements are duly incorporated into the IOTC ROS reporting forms.
